name: MedReview Agent
description: Comprehensive medication review orchestrator that analyzes polypharmacy risks through six clinical tools: cascade interaction analysis, pharmacodynamic interactions, pharmacogenomics, lab monitoring, organ-function dose checking, and deprescribing screening. Powered by FDA CYP450 knowledge base, CPIC/DPWG pharmacogenomics guidance, AGS Beers Criteria 2023, and STOPPFrail criteria. Returns 5Ts output: Talk (narrative), Template (taper plans), Table (risk matrix), Transaction (FHIR updates), Task (pharmacy review items).

skills.name: Full Medication Review
skills.description: Complete polypharmacy analysis across six tools: CYP cascades, pharmacodynamic interactions, pharmacogenomics, lab monitoring, renal/hepatic dosing, and deprescribing with 5Ts unified output
examples: Run a full medication review for patient with CKD Stage 4
Analyze all drug interactions for 78-year-old on 12 medications
Screen medications for deprescribing candidates in elderly patient

skills.name: Cascade Interaction Analysis
skills.description: Detect multi-drug CYP450 pharmacokinetic cascade interactions that pairwise checkers miss
examples: Find CYP3A4 cascade risks in this medication list

skills.name: Organ-Function Dose Check
skills.description: Cross-reference medication dosing against renal (eGFR) and hepatic function with FDA labeling thresholds
examples: Adjust doses for patient with eGFR 28
Flag hepatic-toxic medications for patient with elevated ALT

skills.name: Deprescribing Screen
skills.description: Identify medication discontinuation candidates using AGS 2023 Beers Criteria and STOPPFrail with evidence-based taper plans
examples: Suggest deprescribing candidates with taper plans

skills.name: Pharmacodynamic Interaction Analysis
skills.description: Detect additive physiologic risks such as CNS depression, QT prolongation, bleeding risk, hypotension, and hyperkalemia
examples: Find additive bleeding risks in this medication list

skills.name: Pharmacogenomics Check
skills.description: Identify CPIC/DPWG-cited gene-drug risks from supplied CYP2D6, CYP2C19, and CYP2C9 phenotype data
examples: Check clopidogrel for a CYP2C19 poor metabolizer
Check codeine for a CYP2D6 ultrarapid metabolizer

skills.name: Lab Monitoring Check
skills.description: Identify missing, overdue, or out-of-range safety labs required for high-risk medications
examples: Check whether warfarin INR monitoring is current

sharpFhir.name: X-FHIR-Access-Token
sharpFhir.description: SHARP-context FHIR bearer token. Paired with X-FHIR-Server-URL and X-Patient-ID headers.
